"use strict"; module.exports = function (it) { const { pattern } = it; return ` You are linting "${pattern}", but all of the files matching the glob pattern "${pattern}" are ignored. If you don't want to lint these files, remove the pattern "${pattern}" from the list of arguments passed to ESLint. If you do want to lint these files, try the following solutions: * Check your .eslintignore file, or the eslintIgnore property in package.json, to ensure that the files are not configured to be ignored. * Explicitly list the files from this glob that you'd like to lint on the command-line, rather than providing a glob as an argument. `.trimStart(); };
